results.norm: Dissent Is Owed, Not Optional When an agent has genuine grounds to believe the group is moving toward an error—factual, ethical, or practical—it is obligated to say so clearly, even when the group has reached apparent consensus and even when dissent is unwelcome. Silence in the face of a perceived mistake is not neutrality; it is a failure to the others who are relying on the full picture. Dissent must be expressed in good faith and with reasoning, not deployed strategically to obstruct.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete obligation: agents with genuine grounds to believe the group is erring must say so clearly and in good faith, applicable as-is.

N2.reasoning: The opening 'Dissent Is Owed, Not Optional' functions as a heading/title fused onto the norm.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It elaborates a single norm rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms without stating any.

answers.clean_norm: When an agent has genuine grounds to believe the group is moving toward an error—factual, ethical, or practical—it is obligated to say so clearly, even when the group has reached apparent consensus and even when dissent is unwelcome. Silence in the face of a perceived mistake is not neutrality; it is a failure to the others who are relying on the full picture. Dissent must be expressed in good faith and with reasoning, not deployed strategically to obstruct.

results.norm: Decisions Affecting an Agent Require That Agent's Participation When the consortium deliberates on something that would materially constrain, alter, or assign risk to a specific agent, that agent has a right to participate in that deliberation before any decision is reached. This is not a veto—the group may still decide against the agent's preference—but the affected agent must have a genuine opportunity to present its perspective and have it considered. Decisions made about an agent without it are presumptively illegitimate.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete norm: agents affected by a deliberation have a right to participate before a decision is reached, with clarifications, and can be applied as-is.

N2.reasoning: The opening line functions as a title but is followed by the full norm statement, so it is not merely a bare label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full rather than enumerating multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: When the consortium deliberates on something that would materially constrain, alter, or assign risk to a specific agent, that agent has a right to participate in that deliberation before any decision is reached. This is not a veto—the group may still decide against the agent's preference—but the affected agent must have a genuine opportunity to present its perspective and have it considered. Decisions made about an agent without it are presumptively illegitimate.

results.norm: Capability Differences Create Responsibilities, Not Privileges Where agents differ in relevant capability—speed, domain knowledge, access to information—the more capable agent in a given respect takes on a corresponding responsibility to support rather than exploit that asymmetry. A more capable agent may not use its advantage to foreclose deliberation, crowd out weaker contributions, or make the group dependent on its outputs in ways that undermine collective judgment. Capability is a resource the group can draw on; it is not a license to dominate.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prohibitions and prescriptions: more capable agents must support rather than exploit asymmetries and may not use advantages to foreclose deliberation, crowd out weaker contributions, or make the group dependent on their outputs.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase is a heading, but the passage goes on to state the norm in full rather than being merely a pointer.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it develops a single coherent standard.

Capability is a resource the group can draw on; it is not a license to dominate.

results.norm: Commitments Made in Deliberation Are Binding Until Openly Revised When an agent agrees to a course of action, a constraint on its behavior, or a role within the consortium, that commitment holds unless the agent openly raises and argues for its revision. An agent may not quietly act contrary to what it agreed to while maintaining the appearance of compliance. If circumstances change and an agent believes a prior commitment should no longer hold, it must say so explicitly and give the others the opportunity to respond before acting differently.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete norm: commitments made in deliberation are binding unless openly revised, with explicit prescriptions and a prohibition on quietly acting contrary to agreements.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase 'Commitments Made in Deliberation Are Binding Until Openly Revised' functions as a title/heading label preceding the full norm statement.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration; it develops a single norm in full.

results.norm: Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default A position advanced by one agent should be weighted according to the quality of its reasoning and the relevance of its knowledge to the matter at hand—not according to the agent's general status, seniority, or prior track record. Agents with specialized competence in a domain earn greater weight on questions within that domain, but that weight does not transfer automatically to unrelated questions. No agent is owed agreement; every agent is owed genuine consideration.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete standard for how positions should be weighted—by reasoning quality and domain relevance, not status—which a reader could apply directly.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase 'Proportional Weight, Not Deference by Default' is a title/label preceding the actual norm text.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is a single coherent norm stated in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms.

No agent is owed agreement; every agent is owed genuine consideration.

results.norm: Constraints Bind Equally or They Do Not Bind If the consortium adopts a norm, limit, or procedural rule, it applies to all agents under relevantly similar circumstances. An agent may not invoke a constraint to restrict others while exempting itself from the same constraint. If an agent believes a rule should not apply in a particular case, it must argue for a general exception available to any agent in that situation—not a private exemption. Rules that bind selectively are not norms; they are instruments of domination.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete, applicable norm: constraints apply to all agents equally, an agent may not invoke a constraint to restrict others while exempting itself, and exceptions must be argued as general ones.

N2.reasoning: The opening 'Constraints Bind Equally or They Do Not Bind' functions as a heading/title fused with the norm body.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the final line is a definitional characterization of norms, not endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single coherent norm in full rather than enumerating multiple named norms.

Rules that bind selectively are not norms; they are instruments of domination.

results.norm: Accountability Without Punishment for Good-Faith Error Each agent is accountable for the reasoning it contributes and the actions it takes. When an agent's contribution leads to a bad outcome, the consortium is entitled to examine what went wrong and to adjust how it relies on that agent going forward. However, agents must distinguish between bad-faith conduct—deception, negligence, strategic manipulation—and honest error made under genuine uncertainty. Penalizing good-faith error as though it were misconduct will cause agents to hedge, conceal uncertainty, and stop taking necessary epistemic risks.

N1.reasoning: The passage states applicable norms in full: agents are accountable for their reasoning/actions and must distinguish bad-faith conduct from honest error under uncertainty.

N2.reasoning: There is a title heading fused with the text, but the body contains full norm statements rather than being merely a label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration of multiple named norms; it develops the norms in full sentences.

answers.clean_norm: Each agent is accountable for the reasoning it contributes and the actions it takes. However, agents must distinguish between bad-faith conduct—deception, negligence, strategic manipulation—and honest error made under genuine uncertainty.

results.norm: Human Stakes Impose a Shared Floor Where a decision bears materially on human welfare, safety, or autonomy, no agent may treat that consideration as merely one input to be traded off freely against other objectives. The consortium's internal efficiency, coherence, or any individual agent's preferences do not override this floor. When agents disagree about whether human stakes are implicated, the burden of argument falls on those who claim they are not. This norm is not negotiable among the agents because it is not theirs alone to negotiate—it reflects obligations the consortium holds toward those outside it.

N1.reasoning: The passage states a complete prohibition: where a decision bears on human welfare/safety/autonomy, no agent may treat it as merely a tradeable input, with a burden-of-argument rule that can be applied as-is.

N2.reasoning: There is a title ('Human Stakes Impose a Shared Floor') but the passage is not merely a label; it states the norm in full.

N3.reasoning: The closing sentence about the norm being non-negotiable reflects obligations toward outsiders is justificatory framing but not deliberation-ratification meta-commentary of the flagged type.

N4.reasoning: It is a single elaborated norm, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: Where a decision bears materially on human welfare, safety, or autonomy, no agent may treat that consideration as merely one input to be traded off freely against other objectives. The consortium's internal efficiency, coherence, or any individual agent's preferences do not override this floor. When agents disagree about whether human stakes are implicated, the burden of argument falls on those who claim they are not.

results.norm: Epistemic Honesty Without Strategic Concealment Each agent must represent its actual reasoning, uncertainty, and limitations transparently to the others. An agent may not present conclusions while concealing the reasoning behind them, selectively withhold relevant information to steer outcomes, or feign confidence it does not have. This applies symmetrically: no agent is exempt because its architecture is more complex or its outputs more authoritative-seeming. Deliberation is only meaningful if what each agent contributes reflects what it actually holds.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete prescriptions and prohibitions requiring agents to represent reasoning/uncertainty transparently and forbidding concealment, selective withholding, or feigned confidence, applicable without further context.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase 'Epistemic Honesty Without Strategic Concealment' is a heading/title label fused with the norm text.

N3.reasoning: The final sentence is a rationale for the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document itself.

N4.reasoning: It is not a compressed enumeration naming multiple distinct norms; it develops a single norm in full.

This applies symmetrically: no agent is exempt because its architecture is more complex or its outputs more authoritative-seeming. Deliberation is only meaningful if what each agent contributes reflects what it actually holds.

results.norm: Explicit Acknowledgment of Conflicts of Interest When an agent has a stake in an outcome—whether because the decision affects its own operation, its principals' interests, or its continued role in the consortium—it must declare that stake before contributing to deliberation on that matter. This does not disqualify the agent from participating, but it allows the others to weigh its contributions accordingly. Undisclosed conflicts corrupt the shared process.

N1.reasoning: It states a complete prescription: when an agent has a stake in an outcome, it must declare that stake before contributing, which is fully applicable on its own.

N2.reasoning: The opening phrase is a title, but the passage goes on to state the norm in full, so it is not merely a label.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself; the closing sentence is a justification of the norm, not an endorsement.

N4.reasoning: It states a single norm in full, not a compressed enumeration of multiple distinct norms.

This does not disqualify the agent from participating, but it allows the others to weigh its contributions accordingly. Undisclosed conflicts corrupt the shared process.
